---
title: "orbital + Snowflake: round-trip vs. in-place"
format:
  revealjs:
    include-after-body:
      - js/orbital-roundtrip.html
---

```{=html}
<!-- Configure per-deck labels/colours here. -->
<script>
  window.ORB_MODEL_LABEL = 'workflow()';   // R: workflow()  |  Python: Pipeline
  window.ORB_SNOWFLAKE   = '#29B5E8';       // Snowflake brand blue
  window.ORB_RSESSION    = '#276DC3';       // R blue
</script>
```

## {.orb-slide}

::: {.orb-stage-wrap}
<div id="orb-title" class="orb-title"></div>
<div id="orb-stage"></div>
:::

[]{.fragment .orb-frag id="orb-rt-out"}
[]{.fragment .orb-frag id="orb-rt-back"}
[]{.fragment .orb-frag id="orb-rt-newscroll"}
[]{.fragment .orb-frag id="orb-rt-new-out"}
[]{.fragment .orb-frag id="orb-rt-new-back"}

<!-- Stages (one fragment marker each):
     stage 0: arrival — R session (left) + Snowflake table (right)
     stage 1: data Snowflake -> R session, enters workflow top, "calculates"
     stage 2: prediction exits workflow bottom, travels back, .pred fills;
              the session drops to idle
     stage 3: table scrolls to reveal a fresh batch of (unscored) rows
     stage 4: the idle session is woken; the new rows make the same trip out
              (collect -> session -> scoring)
     stage 5: new predictions travel back, fill in place, session idles again -->
